paper_title: Chert and Quartz Sample Preparation and Sampling Strategy

methods.description: Process for preparing chert and quartz samples for isotope analysis, including crushing, sieving, leaching, and magnetic separation to isolate clean SiO2.

steps.description: Chert pebbles (ranging 4-14 cm, b axis) were crushed, and both chert and sand samples were sieved to 250-850 µm.
steps.searchable_summary: crush and sieve chert and sand samples to specified size range

steps.description: Samples were leached in a HCl/HNO3 mixture (3:1) at 150°C for 1.5 h to dissolve carbonates and iron oxides.
steps.searchable_summary: leach samples in acid mixture to remove carbonates and iron oxides

steps.description: Franz magnetic separation was used to remove magnetic grains, including quartz grains with magnetic inclusions.
steps.searchable_summary: perform magnetic separation to remove magnetic grains

steps.description: Samples were leached three times in a 1% HF/HNO3 mixture for 7, 12, and 24 h at 70°C to remove outer rims of quartz grains.
steps.searchable_summary: leach samples in HF/HNO3 mixture to remove quartz grain rims

steps.description: Aliquots of all 10 etched samples were analyzed for Ne isotopes at the Berkeley Geochronology Center.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze etched samples for Ne isotopes

steps.description: Chert samples were washed with isopropanol to remove fine chert particles.
Aliquots from samples MCH5A and EJC5 were crushed to compare degassing results with uncrushed aliquots.
Samples were encapsulated in a tantalum packet and heated under vacuum using a diode laser micro-furnace at 2-4 heating steps between 450 and 1250°C for 15 min at each temperature step.
steps.searchable_summary: heat samples under vacuum in diode laser micro-furnace

steps.description: Ne isotope measurements used the BGC 'Ohio' system and the procedure described in Balco et al. (2019).
steps.searchable_summary: measure Ne isotopes using BGC 'Ohio' system

steps.description: Amounts of 20-30 g of leached and clean quartz from three quartz samples and three chert samples were processed to separate Be and Al oxides.
steps.searchable_summary: process samples to separate Be and Al oxides

steps.description: These were then analyzed for 10Be/9Be and 26Al/27Al at the Center for Accelerator Mass Spectrometry, Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory.

methods.description: Strategy for collecting samples to measure cosmogenic nuclides, focusing on minimizing post-burial production and understanding sedimentary cycles.

steps.description: Samples were collected from deeply shielded locations to minimize the effects of post-burial production.
Quartz sand and chert pebbles were transported by the Miocene Hazeva system and share a similar exposure history.
steps.searchable_summary: collect quartz sand and chert pebbles with shared exposure history

steps.description: Two individual samples of in situ chert nodules (EJC3 and EJC5) were collected from exposed bedrock outcrops of the Eocene source rock in central Jordan.
steps.searchable_summary: collect in situ chert nodules from Eocene source rock
